summary: Problem q2 is a research-level GL(n+1)×GL(n) Rankin–Selberg / Whittaker function construction (Jacquet–Piatetski-Shapiro–Shalika) with a low solvability score of 20%. There is currently no substantive proof to evaluate: the only tracked issue is a resolved seed/placeholder, and the 'best proof' state shows 100 open issues with no completed argument. The core mathematical task—constructing a single Whittaker vector W on G with the required Rankin–Selberg integral properties—remains entirely unaddressed.
problems: No actual proof attempt exists; the sole issue was a placeholder seed task that was closed without any mathematical content to evaluate.
Central mathematical blocker: the explicit construction of a Whittaker vector W on G satisfying the prescribed local Rankin–Selberg integral identity is missing entirely.
Reported state of '100 open issues' with no resolved substantive issues indicates the problem is at the very start of the pipeline with no scaffolding.
The 20% solvability score reflects genuine research-level difficulty in the non-archimedean local theory of GL(n+1)×GL(n) integrals, not merely a documentation gap.
Problem statement excerpt is truncated, so the precise target identity (conductor normalization, character convention) is not fully captured in the tracking context.
highlights: The problem is well-scoped and grounded in a canonical framework (Jacquet–Piatetski-Shapiro–Shalika), giving a clear literature anchor for any attempt.
The placeholder issue was correctly triaged and closed, so the tracker is clean and not cluttered with false errors.
Standard normalizations are specified (non-archimedean F, ring of integers o, additive character psi of conductor o, unipotent subgroups N_r), providing precise setup for a future construction.

suggested_todos.title: Retrieve and pin the full problem statement, including the exact target Rankin–Selberg integral identity and normalization conventions
Draft an initial proof skeleton: define the Whittaker vector W via the JPSS local integral and state the key lemmas (functional equation, local integral convergence/rationality)
Survey JPSS and Cogdell–Piatetski-Shapiro references to identify the explicit Whittaker vector construction applicable to this local setting
Triage the 100 open issues to separate genuine mathematical gaps from boilerplate placeholders and create a prioritized blocker list
Verify and document the conductor and additive-character conventions to ensure consistency across any proof attempt
